query_normalized: automated detection of algorithmic bias in public code repositories computer science
rationale: The paper focuses on sentiment analysis of code-mixed social media text (Hindi/Bengali-English), which is a distinct construct from the user's question regarding the automated detection of algorithmic bias in public code repositories. The term "code" in the candidate paper refers to linguistic code-switching, whereas the user's question concerns software source code, representing a case of distinct constructs sharing only homonym keywords.
